lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You're back. Any luck in Canifis?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I went back to the place where I first met Vanstrom. He was gone though.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I feared as much. Worry not though. The day will come when we get our revenge on him and the rest of his kind.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Anyway, now that you're back, there was something I was hoping you'd help us with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Now that our base of operations is no longer safe, we're going to need to relocate before the vyrewatch descend on us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems like a wise move. What part do I play?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: To the south of Mort'ton there's an old town known as Burgh de Rott. The place was abandoned but I hear some escapees from Meiyerditch have made their home there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you want me to scout the place out?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Exactly. Head down to Burgh de Rott and see if you can find a suitable place for our new hideout. While you're there, do what you can to forge a good relationship with the townspeople.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get it done.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Be sure to take a weapon of silver with you. You'll find it of great use if vampyres attack. Good luck to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry. I'm not interested.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very well. You know where we are if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Head to Burgh de Rott and see if the town has a suitable location for our new hideout. While you're there, do what you can to forge a good relationship with the townspeople.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: You'll find the town just south of Mort'ton. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Hello. I hear you're doing some important work for Veliaf. You'd best get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Not so good. It's great to see you're helping us out though.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Is something new and exciting happening?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe. Veliaf has asked me to do some work for him. Hopefully it will benefit you all.

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Get out of it! You're not having our food or our blood!

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Get out of it! You ain't comming in here to get our blood or our food!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on earth makes you think I want your food or your blood?

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Hey! Don't try to sweet talk me. I know you want something! But we got nothing! Nothing!

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: What blood we've got, we're keeping for ourselves! You can tell that stinking Vanstrom to go suck a cow's udder!

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: And as for food, why we've barely got enough to feed ourselves. So you ain't having none!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: This is our town! We live here. It's called Burgh de Rott, and you ain't coming in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you all come from?

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: We escaped from the vampyres. They had us trapped in Meiyerditch to drink our blood! Yuck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's Meiyerditch?

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: As if you don't know! It's that dark city to the east where humans are kept like animals to feed those greedy evil vampyres!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like an awful place! Let me in and I'll help you!

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Yeah, okay then eh! No way! You can't trick me like that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would I want your blood?

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Your probably one of them stinking vampyres in disguise! You ain't coming in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want your blood!

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: We don't care. You probably want our food then, so you ain't coming in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would I want your food?

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: You probably want to eat it all and we ain't gonna let you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I don't want to eat your food. I'm plenty healthy enough!

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Well, that's as maybe, but yous probably wants to drink ours blood! And you aint having it and you aint coming in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, goodbye.

sections.title: When using food on the chest

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Blimey, did you see that? [He/She] put some food in the chest for us! [He/She]'s probably not after anything!

sections.title: Speaking to Florin after using food on the chest

lines.speaker: Florin
lines.text: Hey, what did you put in that chest? Is it something tasty, like a nice cooked snail? Or perhaps a well matured piece of cheese with some fresh bread? Ohhh, I bet it's dead tasty...

lines.speaker: Child Citizen
lines.text: Sorry. I shouldn't talk to strangers.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Hey there. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not so bad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come you have no food?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Well obviously we just don't seem to get out to the shops like we used to!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come you have no food?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: We can only stay in the village and there's precious little food in here, just rats to live on! Not very appetising.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: There's also nowhere to store any food around here, the town is incredibly run down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come you have no food?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: We dare not leave the town for fear that the vampyres might spot us and force us to pay a blood tithe.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: We're all very weak and would most likely die if they took more blood from us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: This is our town! We's live here. It's called Burgh de Rott. It aint much to look at, but it's our home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: My name's [citizen name]. I do a bit of this, a bit of that. Just try to survive really, there's nothing much else we can do.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh, my name's [citizen name]. I just help out around here, you know, looking for food and so on.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Not that it makes much difference but my name's [citizen name]. Like all the other survivors here, I just try to make do and get through each day at a time.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh hi. I'm [citizen name]. I hunt rats mostly. Speaking of which, this rat tastes awful, but it's the only food we have!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Huh? I'm [citizen name]. Ravaging after food is what I mostly do. Ocasionally I'll happen across a rat, and that's a good day, just like today!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh, hello. [Citizen name] is the name and I help to keep the village clear of rat infestations. Clearly I love my work, as I bring it home with me... On a stick!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: I'm [citizen name]. I'm just trying to stay warm.

lines.speaker: Elisabeta
lines.text: I'm Elisabeta, the village leader.

lines.speaker: Teodor
lines.text: I'm Teodor. I'm a farmer. Not that there's much to grow here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: I'm Aurel, the village store owner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you open the shop for me please?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: I'm sorry my friend, but I cannot open a store which has no stock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Cornelius
lines.text: I'm Cornelius. I don't really do anything here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Gabriela
lines.text: I'm Gabriela. I'm looking after my husband who isn't feeling too well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are there any 'out of the way' places here?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: How do you mean, 'out of the way'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somewhere where myself and a couple of friends could hold up for a while without attracting any vampyre attention?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Ah, right, I know what you mean. Hmm, let's think now...

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Well, there's the inn. You could look there I suppose. The cellar would have been useful, but the trapdoor down to it is covered with rubble from a broken wall.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: The cellar itself is probably filled with rubble as well. But you may be able to do something with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That could work. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Listen, if you do decide to join us here, we'll expect you to contribute. You know, help to fix things up a bit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Spealing to citizens while clearing the inn
Speaking to citizens after clearing the inn

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there, I'm finished with my project at the inn.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh, that's good to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of your town?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: It's barely a town. I mean, not exactly a breeding ground for high society is it?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: At least we can hide from the vampyres here though. Hopefully we can start to get things up and running soon. You know, some of the trappings of normal life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to help fix up the town.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh, that's very kind of you!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Perhaps you'd consider fixing up the general store. Go and have a chat with Aurel if you want more details.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, sounds interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Hey there. So you're looking to clear out the cellar at the inn? How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not so bad, but it's a lot of hard work to be honest.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh well, I'm sure you can manage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there, I'm finished with my project at the inn.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Oh, that's good to know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Yeah, what can I do ya for?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Hi there. You've done a good job on my store! It's starting to look great!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, but I've only fixed the roof and the walls.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I wonder if I could ask a few questions?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Sure, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: I'm Aurel, the village store owner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you open the shop for me please?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: I'm sorry my friend, but I cannot open a store which has no stock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of your town?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: It's barely a town. I mean, not exactly a breeding ground for high society is it?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: At least we can hide from the vampyres here though. Hopefully we can start to get things up and running soon. You know, some of the trappings of normal life.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: It's looking better since you fixed my store, that's for sure. It would be good to get some stock in as well though.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Just some basic commodities, nothing too fancy like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did everyone come to be here?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: We managed to escape from Meiyerditch where we were being farmed as food for the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meiyerditch?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: That's right. It's part of the Sanguinesti region to the east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to help fix up the town.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Oh, that's very kind of you!

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Perhaps you would consider helping to fix up my general store. It certainly could do with sorting out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What needs fixing?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Please can you fix the roof, then the walls. Once that's done, come and have a chat with me regarding the stores stock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take a look.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Yes I know. You said you'd help fix up my general store!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh! Right, did I? Where was I?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Well, I'm not sure. Could you take a look at the roof and then look at the walls. Once you've done that come and have a chat with me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: I thought you were fixing up my general store? First fix the roof, then the walls. After that, come and have a chat with me.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Well you could stock the store for us. We'd really start looking like a town then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What stock do you need?

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: I need 3 tinderboxes, 10 snails of any type/raw mackerel and 10 bronze axes.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: There you go. You can put them in that.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: You need to collect 10 bronze axes, 10 [snails of any type/raw mackerel] and 3 tinderboxes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Speaking to citizens while fixing the general store

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, I wonder if I could ask a few questions?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Sure, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of your town?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: It's barely a town. I mean, not exactly a breeding ground for high society is it?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: At least we can hide from the vampyres here though. Hopefully we can start to get things up and running soon. You know, some of the trappings of normal life.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: It's coming together now. The store is looking nicer now that it's been fixed up a bit. It would be better with some stock in it though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did everyone come to be here?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: We managed to escape from Meiyerditch where we were being farmed as food for the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meiyerditch?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: That's right. It's part of the Sanguinesti region to the east of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to help fix up the town.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh, that's very kind of you!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Perhaps you'd consider fixing up the general store. Go and have a chat with Aurel if you want more details.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, sounds interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: I thought you were helping Aurel stock his store?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Wow, that's fantastic. Well done! You're doing a great job.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Hey, you've done such a great job for me. Would you consider helping us fix up the bank as well?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can probably look into it.

sections.title: Speaking to citizens while fixing the bank

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Hey it's you! You're doing a great job with the town! It's really getting a lot better!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm happy to help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of your town?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: It's a lot better now that we have a general store, you're doing a great job! What a hero!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh, I think it's going great. We're all so pleased with the work you're doing, but there's still lots left to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: It would be good if you could fix the bank up. I'd suggest starting with the bank booth first.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: You need to fix up the bank. If the booth is done then you should move onto the wall.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Well, the bank is all fixed now, but it isn't going to serve people all on its own. You'll have to recruit someone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Cornelius
lines.text: Well, the bank is all fixed now, but it isn't going to serve people all on its own. You'll have to recruit someone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you fancy the job?

lines.speaker: Cornelius
lines.text: Me? Why, I guess I could... Yes, in fact, I'm sure I could!

lines.speaker: Cornelius
lines.text: I'd best go and get changed now...

sections.title: Fixing the hole in the furnace
Speaking to citizens while fixing the furnace

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Hello there. Thank you so much for all of your work so far. We all really appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Cornelius
lines.text: I'm Cornelius. I run the bank now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think of your town?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Oh it's brilliant, but we could really do with somewhere to melt down the local ores. Maybe you could fix up the furnace?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why does everyone stay in this town?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: It's safer here than anywhere else in Morytania. I guess we could try and escape across the Salve but those horrible ghasts in Mort Myre would probably just eat us alive!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: You've already done quite a lot and we're all super grateful. That said, I overheard someone saying how great it would be if the old furnace were up and working again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Gadderanks.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: My lord... We've finished our latest round of tithing. I'm afraid we haven't been able to collect the desired amount.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I saw your wife today, Gadderanks. She's well.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I hope she stays that way.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: My lord Vanstrom, there are precious few human settlements left worth collecting tithes from.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Mort'ton is just a dead town, full of afflicted. Lord Malak was so ill when he drank of them last time.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Canifis is delivering more than expected but that still isn't sufficient.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Please, where should I get the remainder from?

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: I couldn't care less for your problems Gadderanks. Just do the job you've been appointed to do.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: Wait... Do you smell that? Smoke. It's coming from Burgh de Rott.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: But Burgh de Rott is abandoned.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: No. Burgh de Rott was abandoned. That is clearly no longer the case.

lines.speaker: Vanstrom Klause
lines.text: It seems you have a fresh appointment, Gadderanks. I hope there will be no more delay.

lines.speaker: Gabriela
lines.text: It's Gadderanks! He's found us!

sections.title: Gadderanks arrives at Burgh de Rott
Speaking to citizens after fixing the furnace

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Well done big shot! The smoke from the furnace attracted Gadderanks and his juvinates!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Well done big shot! The smoke from the furnace attracted Gadderanks and his juvinates. They've set up a blood tithing station in the general store!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's Gadderanks?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: He's some human lackie for the vampyres. His job is to collect blood tithes from all the villages around here. He goes around with his juvinate bodyguards because he's too weak to defend himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a juvinate?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: A form of vampyre. They're stronger than juveniles but not as bad as a full-blown vyre. Still, no one that I've seen has ever gotten the better of one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: If I were you, I'd get out of town. Now that Gadderanks is here, I doubt many people will stay around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I can stop him.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Ha! In your dreams. He's right here if you want to give it a go. It's your funeral though!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Ha! In your dreams. He's set up in the general store if you want to give it a go. It's your funeral though!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Oh happy days! It's so good we found all these specimens here! If it hadn't been for some good soul lighting that furnace, we'd have never come down here!

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Now I should be able to make my blood tithe quota... especially after you pay your tithe. You look like an incredibly fit specimen!

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: I bet you could pay twice as much as the others before passing out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have any intentions of paying a blood tithe!

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Ha! We'll see about that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Vampyre Juvinate
lines.text: We're getting some delicious blood tithes from these sacks of food. Yum!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Wiskit
lines.text: They're draining my blood, that's what they're doing!

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: I've had enough of you poking your nose in here! If you think you can stop us, go ahead!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Fear not my friend! I will come to your aid!

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: *cough*

lines.speaker: Veliaf
lines.text: Huh, it looks like Gadderanks is still alive. Perhaps he's ready to give us some information before he meets his maker.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't look too well, Gadderanks. Against my better judgement, I feel sorry for you.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: You... You do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do. I understand that you wanted to feel strong, but you took the wrong side.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: But... you don't understand. I just didn't want my family... I wanted to keep them safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You need to take a second and think of them then. Think of why you did this in the first place. All of that will count for nothing if you don't help us!

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Then... will you do me a favour? Please, try to save them. Please! I beg you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have my word. If we find them, we'll take care of them.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: Thank you. I have some information which may help you...

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: They fear some things. Silver dust and garlic to name a couple. Also outlawed are *cough* -ralander and *cough* -ders eggs. The potion which you make with them is also forbidden.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take it easy Gadderanks. Let's see if we can sort you out.

lines.speaker: Gadderanks
lines.text: It's too late for me *cough*. Though with what I've told you *cough*, perhaps you can find a way to *cough* bring light to Morytania.

lines.speaker: Aurel
lines.text: Hey there! Gadderanks dropped this warhammer, I guess it belongs to you now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew! Thanks for your help Veliaf. It sure was good of you to turn up.

lines.speaker: Veliaf
lines.text: Don't mention it. Have you had any luck with finding us a new hideout?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have. The cellar of the inn here should work perfectly.

lines.speaker: Veliaf
lines.text: Excellent! Now, I must return to the Hollows. Please, meet me there so we can arrange getting everyone down here safely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: First, let Polmafi and Radigad know that it's time for us to depart. After you've done that, please could you escort Ivan to Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Once you're there, Drezel might be able to help you with this Ivandis business.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Please could you escort Ivan to Paterdomus. Once you're there, Drezel might be able to help you with this Ivandis business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you make of what Gadderanks said?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It could be a load of rubbish, but it may be worth looking into. I recognise at least two of the items he mentioned as ingredients for a potion.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The mention of garlic and silver is interesting though. We know vampyres are weak to both. The way he talked though suggests they could be used in a potion as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I test that?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: A good question my friend, but I have no answer for you. Perhaps you could experiment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why does Ivan need to go to Paterdomus?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: He'll be safer there under the eye of Drezel. Drezel can help him further his spiritual skills so that when he is older and wiser, he can rejoin the fight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about Ivandis.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Long ago, the Seven Priestly Warriors drove the vampyre forces out of Misthalin and blessed the Salve so that they'd never be able to return.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Now, the priests of Saradomin would have you believe that all seven are buried beneath Paterdomus. However, I have heard rumour that one of the warriors, Ivandis Seergaze, is actually buried elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where is he buried instead?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: He supposedly led countless charges into Morytania and ultimately perished somewhere within these lands. If that is the case, his followers would likely have buried him near the place he died.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The reason this is important is because it is said that Ivandis wielded a weapon of great power against the vampyres. If he died in Morytania, this weapon may have been buried with him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd best be off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey there. I hear you're going to be taking me to the temple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. I just need to speak with Polmafi and Radigad and then we'll head off.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hmmm, it seems that you have a follower. I think it may get lost on this journey if it's left to wander on its own. Let's leave when your follower is safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey. Veliaf says it's time to head down to the new hideout in Burgh de Rott. Could you let Radigad know as well? I'm taking Ivan to Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Okay. Thanks for letting me know. I'll tell Radigad.

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm just getting my stuff together before leaving. Good luck with your trip to the temple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey. Veliaf says it's time to head down to the new hideout in Burgh de Rott. Could you let Polmafi know as well? I'm taking Ivan to Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Okay. Thanks for letting me know. I'll tell Polmafi.

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm just getting my stuff together before leaving. Good luck with your trip to the temple.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met, friend. Glad you made it back in one piece.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Likewise. So, what happens now?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Do you know of Drezel?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The priest at Paterdomus? Yes, I've helped him out before.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ah, excellent. Well Drezel is an old friend and he's agreed to do us a favour. Ivan is too young and inexperienced to be out here with us. He also aspires to be a priest himself.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Drezel has agreed to take him in and train him. Once he is older and wiser, he can rejoin our fight as a spiritual advisor.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Myself, Polmafi and Radigad will soon be heading down to Burgh de Rott. While we do so, I'd appreciate it if you could escort Ivan to Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I can manage that.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Thank you. There's something else I could use your help with as well.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I'm sure you've noticed by now that our weapons have limited use against the vampyres. Even weapons made of silver only go so far. If we're to have any hope of beating their kind, we'll need to address this problem.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If you've met Drezel, then I've no doubt that he's told you the tale of the Seven Priestly Warriors who blessed the Salve and saved Misthalin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He has indeed.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well I have heard rumour that one of these warriors, a man called Ivandis Seergaze, used a weapon of great power against the vampyres. I have also heard that he isn't actually buried at Paterdomus with the others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. And you'd like me to look into this?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If you could, my friend, you would have my gratitude. Drezel will likely know more, though I find he isn't always forthcoming where rumours are involved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can dig up.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Before you go, do you mind telling Polmafi and Radigad that it's time for us to depart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure.

sections.title: Speaking to Ivan during an encounter

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Let's get of this place... I'm scared!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Did those vampyres say they wanted to 'eat' me?

sections.title: Using food on Ivan during an encounter

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Food is the very last thing on my mind right now... let's get out of here!

sections.title: If Ivan takes too much damage

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: I'm getting badly beaten up here, I'm going to die here if I stay...see you back at the old base.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Many thanks for bringing me safely to the temple. I'm going to rush on ahead as I'm eager to get out of these muddy clothes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a problem. I need to chat to Drezel about some things. I'll see you later.

sections.title: Talking to Ivan before he leaves after finishing the encounter

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Excuse me.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Thanks for the helmet! That's great! It's going to help a lot! I'm going to wear it under my normal clothes.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Thanks for the chainbody! That's great! It's going to help a lot! I'm going to wear it under my normal clothes.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Thanks for the steel legs! That's great! It's going to help a lot! I'm going to wear them under my normal clothes.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Thanks for the kind offer of some armour, but I can only wear a steel medium helm, steel chainbody and steel platelegs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You might find this sickle helpful on our travels.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Many thanks friend, I shall endeavour to wield it as bravely as Veliaf and yourself!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Ohh delicious, some food! I'll keep that for the trip. I could carry another [number] bits of food.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Ohh delicious, some food! I'll keep that for the trip. I'm totally stocked up with food now, thanks!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Thanks for the kind offer of food, but my favourites are either cooked snails, salmon, slimy eel or stew!

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Hey there. I hear you're going to be taking me to the temple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. We should get going soon.

lines.speaker: Ivan Strom
lines.text: Very well. I'm ready, lead the way.

lines.speaker: Vamp Juvinate
lines.text: There's that tasty morsel Ivan! Time for some food...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks very much for your help. I really appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I just hope that all our sacrifices are worth it.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Greetings again adventurer. How go your travels in Morytania? Is it as evil as I have heard?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Well done for bringing Ivan to the temple, [player name]. He will be much safer here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it seems to be more dangerous in Morytania doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Indeed it does my friend. But thanks to you and the Myreque, we have at least the hope of finding salvation in this dark and oppressive land.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Veliaf told me about Ivandis.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Hmmm, how can I say this diplomatically. Veliaf is a good leader, but perhaps he shouldn't be voicing ideas about religious history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you don't even know what he said about Ivandis.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I assume he's made suggestions that Ivandis isn't actually buried in this temple? If he has made such a claim, it's not something I'm prepared to talk about.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you're sure that Ivandis is buried here then?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: If you don't mind me saying, that is a bit of a redundant question. All of the Seven Priestly Warriors are buried here, including Ivandis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where did this rumour that he's buried elsewhere come from then?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Rumours come from all kinds of places. They are rarely true. I am sorry to be so blunt, but I'm offended that you challenge me on this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What proof do you have that Ivandis is buried here?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Servants of Saradomin are not concerned with triffling matters such as proof. We have our faith, and our faith guides us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't actually know for sure that he is buried here?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Please, do not challenge me on matters of faith.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the history of this temple?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I know that it is built on the site where the Seven Priestly Warriors defended Misthalin against the evil vampyres. I also know that their sanctified and holy remains rest peacefully beneath us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you checked that then? Have you seen their remains with your own eyes?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Why would I need to? You'd have me dig up their blessed remains? For what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there somewhere that I might get more information on Ivandis?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: What little information there is, is probably already known to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you have no further information on him that could possibly help me? Anything you know might be of use in freeing the poor people of Morytania.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I have told you all I know. That is as much as I, or anyone, can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you let me check Ivandis' tomb?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Absolutely not! What a disgrace that you should even ask! He rests pure and undisturbed beneath this hallowed earth!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to consider every possible thing which could help us!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I don't see how that can help you! And if you continue in this vein, it will certainly hinder you for you shall not have my support!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you so resistant to talking about history?

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I am not resistant at all, my friend. I would happily talk history with you for as long as you desire. You do not wish to speak of history though, just rumours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you're holding something back! We really need all the help we can get!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I understand that my friend, but digging up the remains of saintly heroes will not aid us in any way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The lives of those pitiful few left in Morytania could rest on this!

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: And you know I would do anything to help those people.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know. So please, help me as much as you can.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Very well, but there is only so much I can do. Remember that I am still a priest of Saradomin.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: I know not if it will help, but please take this key. It will allow you access to a secret library here in the mausoleum. In there you may find what you're looking for, but I pray you never reveal this to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Drezel.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Very well then. Morytania is lost to ignorance.

lines.speaker: Drezel
lines.text: Not ignorance my friend. There are ways to save Morytania from the vampyres. This is not one of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Polmafi Ferdygris at the new hideout

lines.speaker: Polmafi Ferdygris
lines.text: Hey there. It's great of you to do all this work for Veliaf. I've heard him say some good things about you. Keep up the good work and I'm sure you'll be invited to become a full member of the Myreque!

sections.title: Talking to Radigad Ponfit at the new hideout

lines.speaker: Radigad Ponfit
lines.text: Thanks for doing such good work on the new hideout. Of course, we've tidied up a bit as you can see.

sections.title: Talking to Veliaf before finding the tomb

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Thanks for getting Ivan to Paterdomus safely. Let me know if you have any luck finding information on Ivandis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found a library under Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well that can only be good news. I imagine it will contain all sorts of useful information. Do let me know if you find anything in there of note.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where Ivandis' tomb could be?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well if I knew where it was, I'd be there now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well yeah, but maybe you have a rough idea.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I suspect he is buried somewhere in Morytania. He supposedly led countless charges into these lands and ultimately perished while doing so.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: As for where in Morytania, I can't imagine he'd have made it too far from Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Ivandis?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Ivandis Seergaze was one of the Seven Priestly Warriors. Along with the other six, he managed to hold back the vampyres when they attempted to invade Misthalin.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: The seven of them were also responsible for blessing the Salve, preventing the vampyres from ever crossing.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: It is said that Ivandis wielded a great weapon against the vampyres. One that could render them immobile for a short time.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: There are also stories that credit him with the restoring of something called 'Guthix balance'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guthix? Interesting.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Indeed. As the god of balance, it is said that Guthix will always try to ensure that both Saradomin and Zamorak do not gain an upper hand against the other.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Now, why a Saradominist warrior-priest is connected to him, I couldn't say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: After finding the tomb, but before making the rod

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've found Ivandis' tomb.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That's great news! Hopefully you can find something of use in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's just a coffin from what I can tell. There's some sort of rod fused to the top of it though.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: A rod you say? This sounds like it could be the weapon we seek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suspect it is. I don't think there's any way to remove it though.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I hope you are wrong about that. If we cannot break the mould of our historical defeats, we will never be able to stop Drakan.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've found Ivandis' tomb.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: That's great news! Hopefully you can find something of use in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, there's a coffin with some sort of rod fused to the top of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think the rod can be removed. However, I made a clay mould of it so I might be able to make a new one.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Excellent work. Let me know how it goes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know which metal I should use to make the rod?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I do not, I'm afraid. You mentioned that the original rod is still in Ivandis' tomb though. I'm sure studying it will give you a clue as to how it was made.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If that doesn't work, you might be able to learn something at Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what the rod does?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I believe this rod is the weapon that Ivandis used against the vampyres. The stories say that he used to render them immobile for a short time. You might be able to learn more at Paterdomus.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have brought you the Rod of Ivandis!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hmm, well it certainly looks very authentic. But there doesn't seem to be any enchantment on it, either mortal or devine. Perhaps there is a step of the process you have not completed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find out about the process for completing the rod?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If the truth is anywhere, I bet it's at Paterdomus.

sections.title: Using a silvthrill rod on Veliaf

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go. I've managed to recreate the Rod of Ivandis.

sections.title: Giving the half-holy rod to Veliaf

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have brought you the Rod of Ivandis!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hmm, well it certainly looks very authentic, and I can see it's been enchanted. But there doesn't seem to be any divine blessing on the item. Perhaps there is a step of the process you have not completed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where would I find out about the process for completing the rod?

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: If the truth is anywhere, I bet it's at Paterdomus.

sections.title: Using a rod on the well without a rope
Dipping an unholy rod in Salve
Dipping an enchanted rod in Salve
Talking to Veliaf whilst wielding the rod of Ivandis

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have brought you the Rod of Ivandis!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very impressive. Good work.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Hmmm, I can't take it off your hands while you're still using it. If you want to offer it to me, you'll need to unequip it.

sections.title: Bringing the Rod of Ivandis to Veliaf

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well met. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have brought you the Rod of Ivandis!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Very impressive. Good work.

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: I... I don't believe it! You've really done it!

lines.speaker: Veliaf Hurtz
lines.text: Well [player name], you have certainly excelled yourself in this task. I believe you may soon be true Myreque material.
